180	// TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsReparentedAttemptDescendantsBeforeClaim proves
181	// that the startup pre-claim cleanup pass invokes the broadened cwd-based
182	// classifier from ddx-54d9c455: a reparented descendant whose cwd lives under
183	// the configured execution worktree root but whose leaf directory does NOT
184	// carry the `.execute-bead-wt-*` suffix is reaped before the worker attempts
185	// its first ready-execution claim.
186	func TestWorkStartupCleanup_ReapsReparentedAttemptDescendantsBeforeClaim(t *testing.T) {
187		if runtime.GOOS != "linux" {
188			t.Skip("process scanning only available on Linux")
189		}
190	
191		projectRoot, tempRoot := setupWorkStartupCleanupProject(t)
192	
193		// A reparented descendant whose cwd lives under tempRoot but does NOT
194		// contain the explicit `.execute-bead-wt-*` segment in its leaf.
195		descendantCwd := filepath.Join(tempRoot, "reparented-orphan-20260628T170000-feedface")
196		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(descendantCwd, 0o755))
197		require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(descendantCwd, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
198			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
199			BeadID:       "ddx-startup-reparented",
200			AttemptID:    "20260628T170000-feedface",
201			WorktreePath: descendantCwd,
202		}))
203	
204		// Spawn a real process in its own process group with cwd = the reparented
205		// descendant. The startup pre-claim pass must reap it via the broadened
206		// classifier even though the cwd lacks the `.execute-bead-wt-*` segment.
207		sleepCmd := exec.Command("sleep", "3600")
208		sleepCmd.Dir = descendantCwd
209		sleepCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
210		require.NoError(t, sleepCmd.Start())
211		pid := sleepCmd.Process.Pid
212		t.Cleanup(func() {
213			_ = syscall.Kill(-pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
214			_, _ = sleepCmd.Process.Wait()
215		})
216	
217		root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
218		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work", "--json", "--once", "--project", projectRoot)
219		require.NoError(t, err)
220	
221		var res struct {
222			NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
223		}
224		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
225		assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
226	
227		// Reap the leader zombie so the polling check sees the PID disappear.
228		go func() { _, _ = sleepCmd.Process.Wait() }()
229		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
230			return sleepCmd.Process.Signal(syscall.Signal(0)) != nil
231		}, 3*time.Second, 20*time.Millisecond, "startup pre-claim pass must reap the reparented descendant process group")
232	}

243			Title: "lock two",
244		}))
245	
246		blocker := &blockingStartupProcessCleanup{
247			started: make(chan struct{}),
248			release: make(chan struct{}),
249		}
250		runner := newStartupHousekeepingRunner(projectRoot)
251		runner.processCleanup = blocker.Run
252	
253		newWorker := func() *agent.ExecuteBeadWorker {
254			return &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
255				Store: store,
256				Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
257					return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{

261					}, nil
262				}),
263			}
264		}

266		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker-lock"}
267		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
268		var wg sync.WaitGroup
269		errs := make(chan error, 2)
270		runWorker := func() {
271			defer wg.Done()
272			_, err := newWorker().Run(context.Background(), rcfg, agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
273				ProjectRoot:   projectRoot,
274				CleanupRunner: runner,
275				CleanupLog:    io.Discard,
276				Once:          true,
277			})
278			errs <- err
279		}
280	
281		wg.Add(2)
282		go runWorker()
283		<-blocker.started
284		go runWorker()
285	
286		require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
287			return atomic.LoadInt32(&blocker.calls) >= 1
288		}, 2*time.Second, 10*time.Millisecond)
289		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&blocker.maxActive))
290		time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
291		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&blocker.calls), "second worker must skip the process pass while the cleanup lock is held")
292	
293		close(blocker.release)
294		wg.Wait()
295		close(errs)
296		for err := range errs {
297			require.NoError(t, err)
298		}
299	}

459		_ = tempRoot

464		atomic.AddInt32(&r.calls, 1)
465		active := atomic.AddInt32(&r.active, 1)
466		for {
467			prev := atomic.LoadInt32(&r.maxActive)
468			if active <= prev || atomic.CompareAndSwapInt32(&r.maxActive, prev, active) {
469				break
470			}
471		}
472		if r.started != nil {
473			select {
474			case <-r.started:
475			default:
476				close(r.started)
477			}
478		}
479		select {
480		case <-ctx.Done():
481		case <-r.release:
482		}
483		atomic.AddInt32(&r.active, -1)

128	// TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit pins the contract: the locked
129	// pre-dispatch critical section must hold the tracker lock through
130	// SynthesizeCommit. Without this, the original niflheim race re-opens —
131	// CommitTracker takes and releases the lock, then SynthesizeCommit's
132	// `git commit` races against a sibling worker's CommitTracker advancing
133	// HEAD.
134	//
135	// The test exercises the contract by spawning N goroutines that each:
136	//  1. Acquire withTrackerLock
137	//  2. Call commitTrackerLocked + SynthesizeCommit
138	//  3. Release the lock
139	//
140	// and asserting no compare-and-swap errors surface. This mirrors what
141	// Run()'s locked block now does.
142	func TestRun_LockScopeIncludesSynthesizeCommit(t *testing.T) {
143		const n = 8
144		projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, 1)
145		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
146	
147		// Pre-seed each goroutine's distinct dirt file so SynthesizeCommit
148		// always has something to stage, maximising the race window.
149		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
150			path := filepath.Join(projectRoot, fmt.Sprintf("dirt-%d.txt", i))
151			require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte("dirt\n"), 0o644))
152		}
153	
154		var wg sync.WaitGroup
155		errs := make([]error, n)
156		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
157			i := i
158			wg.Add(1)
159			go func() {
160				defer wg.Done()
161				errs[i] = withTrackerLock(projectRoot, "test", func() error {
162					if err := commitTrackerLocked(projectRoot); err != nil {
163						return err
164					}
165					_, err := gitOps.SynthesizeCommit(projectRoot,
166						fmt.Sprintf("chore: checkpoint pre-execute-bead test-%d", i))
167					return err
168				})
169			}()
170		}
171		wg.Wait()
172	
173		for i, e := range errs {
174			if e == nil {
175				continue
176			}
177			msg := e.Error()
178			lower := strings.ToLower(msg)
179			if (strings.Contains(lower, "cannot lock ref") && strings.Contains(lower, " is at ") && strings.Contains(lower, " but expected ")) ||
180				strings.Contains(e.Error(), "cannot lock ref") {
181				t.Fatalf("goroutine %d hit HEAD CAS race inside locked block: %v", i, e)
182			}
183			t.Fatalf("goroutine %d unexpected error: %v", i, e)
184		}
185	
186		// HEAD must have a clean log — no two commits with the same parent
187		// (which would indicate a divergence the lock should have prevented).
188		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--format=%H %P")
189		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(out), "\n") {
190			fields := strings.Fields(line)
191			// Each commit (except root) has exactly one parent; no merges.
192			assert.LessOrEqual(t, len(fields), 2,
193				"unexpected merge / multi-parent commit: %s", line)
194		}
195	}

143				ActualHoldMS: held.Milliseconds(),

146			})
147		}

149		Emit(Event{

151			LockName:   lockName,
152			Operation:  operation,
153			HolderPID:  pid,
154			AcquiredAt: acquiredAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
155			Severity:   "error",
156			CapMS:      cfg.Cap.Milliseconds(),

158		})

61		// Cap is the maximum hold time. Zero or negative disables enforcement.
62		Cap time.Duration
63		// LockPath is the filesystem path force-released (os.RemoveAll) when the
64		// cap is exceeded. Empty skips force-release.
65		LockPath string
66		// EvidenceDir receives lock-violation.json when the cap is exceeded.
67		// Empty skips the evidence record.
68		EvidenceDir string
69	}

71	// Violation records a lock held past its configured cap. It is written to
72	// <EvidenceDir>/lock-violation.json so the post-execution reviewer sees the
73	// over-long hold even though the worker continues.
74	type Violation struct {
75		LockName     string `json:"lock_name"`
76		CapMS        int64  `json:"cap_ms"`
77		ActualHoldMS int64  `json:"actual_hold_ms"`
78		HolderPID    int    `json:"holder_pid"`
79		Stack        string `json:"stack"`
80	}

82	// InstrumentCapped behaves like Instrument but additionally enforces a
83	// hold-time cap. It emits an "acquire" event, runs critical, and emits a
84	// matching "release" event. If cfg.Cap > 0 and the hold exceeds the cap, a
85	// watchdog fires once: it captures a stack trace, force-releases the lock by
86	// removing cfg.LockPath, writes <cfg.EvidenceDir>/lock-violation.json, and
87	// emits an error-severity "violation" event via the metric helper.
88	//
89	// The critical section is NOT interrupted — it runs to completion
90	// (release-only behaviour); the forced release and violation record are the
91	// loud signal that a hold ran too long. critical's error is returned
92	// unchanged.
93	func InstrumentCapped(lockName, operation string, cfg CapConfig, critical func() error) (err error) {
94		pid := os.Getpid()
95		acquiredAt := time.Now()
96		Emit(Event{
97			Event:      "acquire",
98			LockName:   lockName,
99			Operation:  operation,
100			HolderPID:  pid,
101			AcquiredAt: acquiredAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339Nano),
102		})
103	
104		var timer *time.Timer
105		if cfg.Cap > 0 {
106			timer = time.AfterFunc(cfg.Cap, func() {
107				enforceCapViolation(lockName, operation, pid, acquiredAt, cfg)
108			})
109		}

+// TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace is the regression test for
+// concurrent `ddx work` against the same project hitting
+// "fatal: cannot lock ref 'HEAD': is at X but expected Y" CAS failures during
+// the pre-execute-bead checkpoint.
+//
+// Before the fix, Run() called CommitTracker (locked) → SynthesizeCommit
+// (unlocked) → resolveBase (unlocked) → WorktreeAdd (unlocked). When two
+// workers raced, worker B's CommitTracker advanced HEAD between worker A's
+// `git add` and `git commit` inside SynthesizeCommit, and A's commit failed
+// the HEAD compare-and-swap.
+//
+// After the fix, the entire pre-dispatch sequence runs inside one
+// withTrackerLock acquisition, so concurrent workers serialize through it.
+func TestExecuteBead_ConcurrentWorkers_NoHEADRefRace(t *testing.T) {
+	const n = 5
+	projectRoot, _ := newScriptHarnessRepo(t, n)
+
+	// Each goroutine gets its own directive file (no-op directive — we don't
+	// care about the agent's output, only about the parent-repo
+	// pre-dispatch sequence racing).
+	dirFiles := make([]string, n)
+	tmp := t.TempDir()
+	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
+		dirFile := filepath.Join(tmp, fmt.Sprintf("directive-%d.txt", i+1))
+		writeDirectiveFile(t, dirFile, []string{})
+		dirFiles[i] = dirFile
+	}
+
+	// Build runners up front: NewRunner is not goroutine-safe during catalog
+	// construction.
+	runners := make([]AgentRunner, n)
+	rcfgs := make([]config.ResolvedConfig, n)
+	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
+		runners[i] = NewRunner(Config{})
+		rcfgs[i] = config.NewTestConfigForBead(config.TestBeadConfigOpts{
+			Harness: "script",
+			Model:   dirFiles[i],
+		}).Resolve(config.CLIOverrides{})
